sections.paragraph:
ἐπερώτημα, ατος, τό (s. prec. entry; Hdt. et al.; ins, pap; Da 4:17 Theod.; Just., D. 45, 1).

① the content of asking, question (Hdt. 6, 67; Thu. 3, 53, 2; 3, 68, 1; Sir 33:3 v.l.; Just., D. 45, 1) ξένον ἐ. strange question PEg2 64. λαλεῖ αὐτοῖς κατὰ τὰ ἐ. αὐτῶν according to their questions Hm 11:2.

② a formal request, appeal (ἐπερωτάω 2) συνειδήσεως ἀγαθῆς ἐ. εἰς θεόν an appeal to God for a clear conscience 1 Pt 3:21. But cp. a pledge (s. L-S-J-M s.v. 3 with pap ref.; also the vb. in PYadin 17, 38) to God proceeding fr. a clear conscience (so GRichards, JTS 32, ’31, 77 and ESelwyn, 1 Pt ad loc.); cp. also BReicke, The Disobed. Spirits and Christian Baptism ’46, 182–86; NClausen-Bagge, Eperotaema ’41. DTripp, ET 92, ’81, 267–70, argues for a liturgical ‘stipulatio’ or injunction urging the baptismal candidate to turn sincerely to God’s way.—M-M. TW. Spicq.

prec. prec. = preceding

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Theod. Theod. = Theodotion, revision of Greek OT, II a.d.—List 2, beg.

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Hm Hm = Mandates

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

L-S-J-M L-S-J-M = (L-S-J) HLiddell/RScott, A Greek-English Lexicon; New Edition by HSJones/RMcKenzie—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

ref. ref. = reference(s)

PYadin PYadin = The Documents from the Bar Kokhba Period named in honor of YYadin (a.k.a. PBabatha)—List 4

  1. fr. = from

JTS JTS = Journal of Theological Studies—List 6

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

ET ET = Expository Times—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 362
